Nitrates are used to treat angina pectoris, a condition characterized by chest pain or discomfort due to inadequate blood flow to the heart. Nitrates work by relaxing the smooth muscles in the walls of the arteries, including those that supply blood to the heart. This action reduces the workload on the heart and decreases the need for oxygen-rich blood, thereby lowering the risk of angina pectoris. Nitrates come in various forms, including oral tablets, sublingual tablets, sprays, and creams. Oral tablets are usually taken four times a day, while sublingual tablets are placed under the tongue and dissolved there. Creams are applied to the skin, and sprays are sprayed onto the skin.
